mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show positive mean direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear periods with a t-stat > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a decline in the index of industrial production signals capacity constraints and slowing output, which compresses profit margins and generates negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of late-cycle observations, or the mechanism loses predictive power when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payroll additions reflect robust economic activity and consumer spending power, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive short-term market momentum.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of months, or rolling out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds the 205.0 index threshold, the Federal Reserve commits to aggressive tightening, raising real discount rates and suppressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >55% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling housing starts indicates a shift from residential to non-residential demand, creating sector rotation that suppresses broad market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-HOUST divergence yields positive 20-day returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery environments, accelerating industrial production signals rapid capacity utilization and earnings rebounds, driving immediate positive equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of recovery-phase observations, or the predictive coefficient flips sign during stable growth periods.
